title: rubus × ambiguus
intro: Rubus × ambiguus, also known as the hybrid raspberry or black raspberry, is a plant species derived from the cross-breeding of Rubus idaeus and Rubus occidentalis. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots are used in herbal medicine, exhibiting antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus × ambiguus include its ability to support cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and provide anti-cancer properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or supplement, with the leaves often being used fresh in salads or as a flavoring agent.
